questions.question: Your N8N workflow processes 5000 records from a database. The workflow times out after 500. Which node solves this?
A webhook trigger in N8N receives data but the next node shows empty fields. What is the most likely cause?
You need to call an API that requires Bearer token auth in N8N. Where do you set the Authorization header?
options: In the HTTP Request node under Headers
In the Set node before the request

questions.question: Which N8N node lets you run custom JavaScript code inside a workflow?
A workflow must send Slack messages only when order value exceeds $500. Which node implements this condition?
Your N8N webhook is accessible locally but external services cannot reach it. What is the correct fix?
options: Use ngrok or expose via reverse proxy

questions.question: An API returns paginated results — 100 items per page, 10 pages total. How do you collect all 1000 items in N8N?
options: Use a Loop node with page increment and Merge at end
Use Split In Batches on the first response

questions.question: What does the Merge node's 'Merge By Key' mode do in N8N?
options: Combines all items into one array
Matches items from two branches by a shared field value

questions.question: You want N8N to retry a failed HTTP request up to 3 times with a 5-second delay. Where is this configured?
options: In the HTTP Request node under 'On Error' settings
In the Code node before the request

questions.question: Which expression retrieves the output of a previous node named 'Get User' in N8N?
A workflow runs every night at midnight UTC. Which trigger do you use?
Your N8N workflow sends an email for each item returned by a database query. 200 records came in — 200 emails sent. How do you limit to 1 summary email?
options: Use Aggregate node then send once

questions.question: What is the correct way to pass dynamic data into a URL parameter in an N8N HTTP Request node?
options: Use a Set node to pre-build the URL
Use the Query Parameters section with expression mode

questions.question: N8N shows 'Execution failed: ECONNREFUSED'. What does this mean?
options: The target server refused the connection — it may be down or wrong port

questions.question: You need to transform an array of objects — adding a 'fullName' field combining firstName and lastName. Which node is most efficient?
options: HTTP Request to an external service

questions.question: How do you share credentials (API keys) between multiple workflows in N8N without re-entering them?
options: Use N8N Credentials Manager — create once, reference everywhere
Store in a Set node at workflow start
Use environment variables in each node

questions.question: A Switch node has 4 outputs. What happens to items that match none of the defined rules?
options: They go to the 'fallback' or default output
They loop back to the start

questions.question: Which N8N feature lets you test a webhook without waiting for an external system to send data?
options: The 'Listen for Test Event' button in the Webhook node

questions.question: You deploy N8N on a VPS but the UI is only accessible on localhost. What environment variable exposes it externally?
An N8N workflow calls OpenAI. You want to log the full AI response to Google Sheets AND send it to Slack simultaneously. How?
options: Use two sequential HTTP Request nodes
Connect the OpenAI node output to both Sheets and Slack nodes in parallel
Use a Merge node then split

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Item Lists' node in N8N?
options: Converts multiple items into a single item or splits one item into many

questions.question: A workflow must wait 24 hours before sending a follow-up email. Which node handles this?
In N8N Cloud, where are execution logs stored?
options: N8N's execution database, viewable in the Executions tab

questions.question: Your workflow has an Error Trigger node. When does it activate?
Which N8N node is used to call another workflow from within the current workflow?
A Make.com scenario receives 500 items from a webhook but only processes 100. What setting controls this?
You receive a 429 error from an API in Make.com. What is the correct fix?
options: Add an Error Handler and stop
Add a Sleep module before the API call

questions.question: A Router module in Make.com has 3 routes. What happens to items that match none of the route filters?
options: They are processed by all routes
They go to the last route by default

questions.question: You want to combine all array items into a single CSV string in Make.com. Which module do you use?
What is the difference between a Webhook (Custom) and an Instant Trigger in Make.com?
options: Webhook (Custom) listens for HTTP POST; Instant Trigger is app-specific and uses OAuth push
Instant triggers only work with premium apps

questions.question: A Make.com scenario maps a field but shows 'undefined'. The source module ran successfully. What is the most likely cause?
options: The module hasn't been run once to detect structure — click 'Run once' to map fields

questions.question: How do you handle an API error in Make.com so the scenario continues processing remaining items instead of stopping?
options: Add an Error Handler module set to 'Resume'
Use a Router before the API call

questions.question: Your Make.com scenario needs to process each email attachment separately. Which module splits them?
What is the maximum scheduling frequency on Make.com's free plan?
You need to call an OpenAI API inside Make.com. The response contains JSON inside a string field. How do you parse it?
options: Use a Router to split the string

questions.question: A Make.com scenario must run only on weekdays at 9 AM. How do you configure this?
options: Use Advanced Scheduling with day-of-week filter
Create 5 separate scenarios for each weekday
Use a Router to check the day

questions.question: What does 'operations' mean in Make.com billing?
options: Number of modules executed across all scenario runs

questions.question: You want to send a Slack message only if a Google Sheets row has Status = 'Approved'. Where do you add this condition?
options: In the Google Sheets module settings
As a Filter between the Sheets module and Slack module

questions.question: An HTTP module in Make.com returns a response body as a string. How do you access nested JSON fields like response.data.id?
options: First parse with JSON Parse module, then map the field
Use a Text module to extract it
Not possible without a custom app

questions.question: What happens when a Make.com scenario hits an unhandled error mid-execution?
options: It retries automatically 3 times then stops
It stops at the failed module and logs the incomplete execution
It skips the failed item and continues
It sends an email to the account owner

questions.question: Which Make.com feature allows you to reprocess a failed execution after fixing the scenario?
You need to merge data from two separate API calls into one record in Make.com. Which approach works?
options: Use two HTTP modules and map fields from both into the next module
Use a Router with two paths
Not possible — each module has isolated data

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Set Variable' module in Make.com?
options: Stores a value accessible across all modules in the scenario
Creates a global variable across all scenarios
Replaces data in an external app

questions.question: A webhook scenario in Make.com is not receiving data. The external service shows successful sends. What do you check first?
options: Whether the scenario is active (turned on) and the webhook URL is correctly copied

questions.question: How do you prevent sensitive data (like API keys) from appearing in Make.com execution logs?
options: Use the 'Sensitive Data' toggle on the module — marks fields as hidden in logs
Delete execution logs after each run

questions.question: In Make.com, what does the 'Rollback' error handling directive do?
options: Reverts all changes made in that execution (for supported apps) and stops

questions.question: You need to transform a date from 'MM/DD/YYYY' to 'YYYY-MM-DD' in Make.com. Which approach is correct?
options: Use the formatDate() function in a Set module
Use a Text module with regex

questions.question: What is the correct way to test a Make.com webhook scenario without a real trigger?
options: Use a Manual Trigger module at the start
Click 'Run once' — Make.com listens for one incoming webhook request for testing
Duplicate the scenario and use a different trigger
Make.com does not support webhook testing

questions.question: A scenario processes 1000 rows from Google Sheets. You want to stop after the first row where Status = 'Error'. Which module achieves this?
options: Break directive in an Iterator with error filter

questions.question: How do you share a Make.com scenario template with another team member who has a different Make account?
options: Export as JSON and import on their account
Share via the Make.com Team feature

modules.title: What are AI Agents & Multi-Agent Systems

questions.question: An AI agent answers questions incorrectly because its training data is outdated. Which architecture solves this without retraining?
options: RAG — retrieve current documents at query time and inject into context

questions.question: What is 'function calling' in the context of OpenAI GPT models?
options: Running Python functions inside the LLM
Allowing the LLM to output structured JSON to trigger defined external functions
Calling the API multiple times per request
A deprecated feature replaced by plugins

questions.question: Your RAG system retrieves 10 chunks but the answer is still wrong. Which metric helps diagnose whether relevant chunks are being retrieved?
options: Context recall — measures if the correct chunks appear in retrieved results

questions.question: What is the key difference between a vector embedding and a keyword search index?
options: Embeddings capture semantic meaning, keyword search matches exact terms

questions.question: In a multi-agent system, what is the role of an 'orchestrator' agent?
options: It stores memory for all agents
It coordinates tasks between specialist agents and decides which agent handles each subtask
It embeds documents into a vector database
It evaluates the final output quality

questions.question: You want an AI agent to remember a user's preferences across sessions. Which approach is correct?
options: Store preferences in an external database, retrieve and inject into system prompt at session start

questions.question: What does 'hallucination' mean in LLM outputs and how does RAG reduce it?
options: Confidently wrong answers — RAG grounds responses in retrieved source documents
Token limit errors — RAG compresses context
Output formatting errors — RAG structures responses

questions.question: Which chunking strategy is best for a document where meaning spans multiple paragraphs?
options: Semantic chunking with overlap between chunks

questions.question: An agentic workflow loops indefinitely — the agent keeps calling tools without reaching a final answer. What is the fix?
options: Add a maximum iteration limit and a termination condition check

questions.question: What is 'temperature' in an LLM and when should you set it to 0?
options: Processing speed — set to 0 for accuracy
Randomness of output — set to 0 for deterministic, consistent answers (e.g. data extraction)
Memory allocation — set to 0 to disable caching

questions.question: You embed 10,000 documents into Pinecone. A user queries 'AI regulations in EU'. The retrieval returns irrelevant results. What is the most likely cause?
options: The embedding model used for documents differs from the one used for the query
Too many documents in the index

questions.question: What is 'ReAct' prompting in agentic AI?
options: A React.js framework for AI apps
A prompting pattern where the agent reasons step-by-step then acts, alternating Thought and Action

questions.question: Your AI agent must choose between 3 specialist sub-agents. Which pattern is most appropriate for dynamic routing?
options: Use an LLM router that reads the task description and selects the appropriate agent
Always use all 3 agents and merge results

questions.question: What is the purpose of a 'system prompt' in an AI agent?
options: Sets the agent's identity, constraints, and behavioral rules for the session

questions.question: When should you fine-tune an LLM instead of using RAG?
options: When you need to change the model's writing style, format, or domain-specific reasoning patterns
When you have more than 1000 documents
When RAG latency is too high

questions.question: What does 'context window' mean and why does it matter for agents?
options: The UI window showing the conversation
Maximum tokens the model can process in one call — limits how much history/context you can include

questions.question: An agent uses tool calls in a loop. After 5 iterations it should stop even if not complete. How do you implement this?
options: Add a counter variable and a conditional check before each tool call

questions.question: What metric measures whether an AI agent's final answer is supported by the retrieved context?
options: Faithfulness — checks if the answer is grounded in the retrieved documents

questions.question: You want your agent to use a calculator tool for math instead of relying on the LLM. What is the correct implementation approach?
options: Include math instructions in the system prompt
Define a 'calculate' function, register it as a tool, and let the LLM call it via function calling
Pre-compute all math and hard-code results

questions.question: What is the risk of setting chunk_overlap too high in RAG document chunking?
options: Duplicate content appears in retrieved results, wasting context window space

questions.question: Which database type is optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional embedding vectors?
options: Pinecone, Weaviate, or pgvector — purpose-built vector databases

questions.question: What does 'streaming' mean in LLM API responses and when is it useful?
options: Receiving token-by-token output as generated, enabling real-time display in chat UIs

questions.question: An agentic pipeline must handle both text and image inputs. Which OpenAI model supports this?
options: GPT-4o — supports both text and vision inputs in the same call

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'guardrails' in production AI agents?
options: Input/output validation to prevent harmful, off-topic, or policy-violating responses

questions.question: Your multi-agent system has Agent A pass results to Agent B. Agent B returns an error. How should a robust orchestrator handle this?
options: Retry Agent B with modified input, log the failure, and fall back to a default result if retries fail

questions.question: A Docker container starts but exits immediately with code 1. How do you diagnose the issue?
options: Run: docker logs <container_id> to see the error output
Rebuild with a different base image

questions.question: Your AWS Lambda function times out processing large AI model responses. What is the correct fix?
options: Increase the Lambda timeout (max 15 min) and memory allocation

questions.question: What is the key difference between AWS Lambda and a traditional EC2 server?
options: Lambda runs code only when triggered (pay-per-execution), EC2 runs continuously (pay-per-hour)

questions.question: You deploy an AI app on Modal and need persistent storage for model weights between invocations. Which Modal feature do you use?
options: Modal Volumes — persistent network storage attached to functions

questions.question: Your Docker image is 8GB because it includes the full PyTorch library. How do you reduce it?
options: Use a smaller base image (python:3.11-slim) and install only required packages

questions.question: An IAM policy must allow a Lambda function to read from S3 but nothing else. Which principle guides this?
options: Least Privilege — grant only the minimum permissions required

questions.question: What is the purpose of AWS API Gateway in an AI deployment?
options: Provides a managed HTTPS endpoint, request throttling, and auth for Lambda functions

questions.question: Your CI/CD pipeline deploys to production automatically on every git push. A bad commit breaks production. What process prevents this?
options: Add automated tests and a staging environment — production only deploys on passing tests

questions.question: What is 'cold start' in serverless functions and how do you reduce it for AI apps?
options: Delay when a new function instance initializes — reduce by keeping functions warm or using provisioned concurrency

questions.question: You need to store user chat history for an AI app. The data is unstructured and schema may evolve. Which database is most appropriate?
options: Supabase (PostgreSQL with JSON support) or MongoDB

questions.question: A Modal deployment fails with 'OOM killed'. What does this mean and how do you fix it?
options: Out of Memory — increase the memory parameter in @app.function(memory=...)

questions.question: What is the purpose of environment variables in cloud deployments vs hardcoding values?
options: They separate configuration from code — secrets stay out of source control and can change per environment

questions.question: Your AWS costs spike unexpectedly. CloudWatch shows Lambda invocation count is 100x normal. What is the most likely cause?
options: A recursive Lambda trigger — the function is invoking itself in a loop

questions.question: What does 'container orchestration' solve that running a single Docker container does not?
options: Automatic scaling, self-healing, load balancing, and rolling deployments across multiple containers

questions.question: You want to run a GPU-accelerated AI model on Modal. What do you specify in the function decorator?
What is the difference between horizontal and vertical scaling in cloud?
options: Horizontal = add more instances; Vertical = give each instance more CPU/RAM
Horizontal = more RAM; Vertical = more instances

questions.question: A Supabase row-level security (RLS) policy is enabled but all queries return no rows. What is the most likely cause?
options: No RLS policy has been created for the table — RLS blocks all access until policies are defined

questions.question: What does a Dockerfile's ENTRYPOINT instruction define?
options: The command that runs when the container starts

questions.question: You need to send 10,000 AI processing jobs to Modal without overwhelming it. Which pattern is correct?
options: Use Modal's .map() or Queue to process concurrently within configured limits
Process one at a time sequentially

questions.question: What is 'blue-green deployment' in CI/CD?
options: Running two identical production environments — route traffic to green after validating, then decommission blue

questions.question: Your AI app's API latency is 3 seconds. 80% of requests ask the same questions. How do you reduce latency?
options: Add Redis caching — cache common responses, return cached result in <50ms

questions.question: What is the purpose of a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) in AWS?
options: Isolates your cloud resources in a private network, preventing external access to internal services

questions.question: A secret API key is accidentally committed to a public GitHub repo. What is the immediate correct action?
options: Rotate/revoke the key immediately — assume it is compromised — then remove from git history

questions.question: In Modal, what does @modal.concurrent(max_inputs=50) do?
options: Allows each container instance to handle up to 50 simultaneous requests

questions.question: What is the role of a load balancer in a cloud deployment?
options: Distributes incoming traffic across multiple server instances to prevent overload

questions.question: Two VLANs (10 and 20) are on the same switch but cannot communicate. What is required for inter-VLAN routing?
options: A Layer 3 switch or router with subinterfaces (router-on-a-stick)
Configure the same IP subnet for both VLANs

questions.question: BGP neighbor adjacency is stuck in 'Active' state. What does this indicate?
options: The router is trying to connect but cannot reach the neighbor — check IP, ACL, or BGP port 179

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of SD-WAN over traditional WAN?
options: Centralized policy management, application-aware routing, and ability to use multiple links (MPLS + internet) intelligently

questions.question: A Zero Trust architecture rejects the idea of a trusted internal network. What does it enforce instead?
options: Verify every user and device on every request regardless of location — never trust, always verify

questions.question: A host cannot reach the internet but can ping the default gateway. Which OSI layer is the most likely problem?
options: Layer 3 (Network) — routing or NAT issue above the gateway

questions.question: What does a NAT overload (PAT) do that basic NAT does not?
options: Translates one private IP to one public IP
Maps multiple private IPs to one public IP using port numbers to distinguish sessions

questions.question: OSPF neighbors are stuck in EXSTART state. What is the most common cause?
options: MTU mismatch between the two interfaces

questions.question: You write a Python Netmiko script to push config to 50 routers sequentially. It takes 25 minutes. How do you speed this up?
options: Use concurrent.futures or asyncio to connect to multiple routers simultaneously

questions.question: What is the difference between a stateful and stateless firewall?
options: Stateful tracks connection state and allows return traffic automatically; stateless evaluates each packet independently

questions.question: A subnet needs to host exactly 30 devices. Which subnet mask wastes the fewest IPs?
options: Both /26 and /27 work — use /27 for less waste

questions.question: What does SNMPv3 provide that SNMPv1 does not?
options: Authentication and encryption — SNMPv1 sends community strings in plaintext

questions.question: An MPLS label is added to a packet. At which OSI layer does this occur?
You need to automate parsing of 'show ip route' output from 100 Cisco routers. Which Python library is best suited?
options: TextFSM or pyATS — built for parsing structured CLI output from network devices

questions.question: What problem does Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) solve?
options: Layer 2 switching loops — prevents broadcast storms by blocking redundant paths

questions.question: A site-to-site IPsec VPN tunnel is down. Phase 1 is up but Phase 2 fails. What should you check?
options: Verify the IPsec transform set (encryption/hash algorithms) and ACL/traffic selectors match on both sides

questions.question: What is the purpose of NetFlow in network monitoring?
options: Exports flow records (source/dest IP, ports, byte counts) for traffic analysis and bandwidth monitoring

questions.question: BGP route reflector eliminates the need for what?
options: Full mesh iBGP — the reflector distributes routes to clients without requiring all routers to peer with each other

questions.question: You use Ansible to configure network devices. A playbook runs but makes no changes. What most likely explains this?
options: Idempotency — the device config already matches the desired state, so Ansible made no changes

questions.question: What is microsegmentation in a Zero Trust network?
options: Splitting the network into smaller subnets
Applying granular security policies between individual workloads — even east-west traffic is controlled

questions.question: A Grafana dashboard shows interface utilization at 95% for 6 hours. What is the correct engineering response?
options: Ignore it — occasional spikes are normal
Investigate the traffic source, consider QoS policies, and plan capacity upgrade if sustained

questions.question: What does 'route summarization' achieve in OSPF or BGP?
options: Reduces the number of routes advertised — smaller routing tables, less memory and CPU on routers

questions.question: Which Python library lets you send crafted network packets (e.g. custom TCP/ICMP) for testing?
A wireless client gets IP 169.254.x.x. What does this indicate?
options: APIPA address — the client failed to get a DHCP response and self-assigned a link-local address

questions.question: What is the key difference between OSPF and BGP?
options: OSPF is an interior gateway protocol for within an AS; BGP is an exterior gateway protocol used between autonomous systems (the internet's routing protocol)
No meaningful difference for enterprise networks

questions.question: You need to collect logs from 50 network devices and alert when error rate exceeds a threshold. What stack achieves this?
options: Syslog → Elasticsearch/Loki → Grafana with alerting rules

modules.title: Tool Selection: N8N vs Make vs Custom

questions.question: A client spends 40 hours/week on data entry that could be automated. The automation costs $5,000 to build. Staff cost $20/hr. What is the break-even point?
A client wants AI automation but their data is stored in 5 different unconnected systems. What must be addressed first?
options: Data integration — connect or consolidate data sources before automation can work reliably

questions.question: When should you recommend N8N over Make.com for a client?
options: When the client needs self-hosted deployment for data privacy, has technical staff, or requires custom code nodes
When cost is the only concern
When the client uses Google Workspace

questions.question: A client asks 'What is the ROI of implementing AI in our business?' What information do you need to calculate this?
options: Current process cost (time × rate), implementation cost, ongoing maintenance, and measurable output improvement
The AI vendor's published ROI stats

questions.question: A client's CEO is enthusiastic about AI automation but department heads are resistant. What is the correct consulting approach?
options: Address change management — involve department heads early, show quick wins, and demonstrate how automation helps rather than replaces them

questions.question: What should a consulting proposal scope document always include to protect both parties?
options: Deliverables, timeline, assumptions, out-of-scope items, payment terms, and revision limits

questions.question: A client wants to automate customer support with AI. They get 500 tickets/day, 80% are repetitive questions. What metric do you track post-implementation?
options: Deflection rate — % of tickets resolved without human intervention, and customer satisfaction score (CSAT)

questions.question: You discover mid-project that the client's data quality is too poor for the AI system to work. What do you do?
options: Continue and deliver a poor result
Stop, document the finding in a change request, propose a data cleanup phase with revised timeline and cost

questions.question: Which process is best suited for AI automation: a creative brainstorming session or a monthly invoice reconciliation process?
options: Invoice reconciliation — it is rule-based, repetitive, and high-volume with measurable outcomes
Neither — AI cannot automate business processes

questions.question: A client insists on automating a process that involves frequent exceptions and human judgment. What is your recommendation?
options: Implement assisted automation — AI handles the standard cases, flags exceptions for human review

questions.question: What is the difference between AI automation consulting and software development consulting?
options: AI automation consulting focuses on process identification, tool selection, and business ROI; software dev focuses on building custom systems from scratch

questions.question: A client has a $10,000 budget for AI automation. Their top 3 processes could each save $8,000/year. How do you prioritize?
options: Automate the highest-value, lowest-complexity process first to deliver quick ROI and build trust
Let the client decide without guidance

questions.question: What is a 'process map' and why is it essential before building automation?
options: A geographic map of office locations
A visual diagram of every step, decision point, and actor in a business process — ensures automation mirrors reality, not assumptions

questions.question: A client wants to use AI to generate sales proposals automatically. What risk must you highlight?
options: AI may generate inaccurate pricing or commitments — human review and approval gates are required before sending
The client's CRM may not support it

questions.question: You are presenting an AI solution to a non-technical CEO. Which approach is most effective?
options: Lead with business outcomes (cost saved, hours reduced, revenue impact) and show a live demo of the working solution
Use technical jargon to establish credibility

questions.question: What does 'pilot project' mean in AI consulting and why is it recommended?
options: A test flight — unrelated to consulting
A small-scale implementation of the automation on one process or team — validates the approach before full rollout, reducing risk
A free project for the client

questions.question: A Myanmar SME client has no IT infrastructure. They want AI automation. Where do you start?
options: Assess their digital maturity first — tools they currently use, data availability, team tech literacy — then recommend appropriate entry-level automation (e.g. Make.com + Google Sheets)
Tell them they are not ready

questions.question: What is a key sign that a business process is NOT ready for automation?
options: The process has no consistent rules — outcomes depend heavily on human judgment, relationships, or informal knowledge

questions.question: A client wants to know how their AI chatbot is performing. Which KPIs do you track?
options: Containment rate, user satisfaction score, escalation rate, and average resolution time

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a retainer consulting model and a project-based model?
options: Retainer = ongoing monthly fee for continued support and improvements; Project = fixed scope and price for a defined deliverable

questions.question: A client asks you to automate a process that handles personal data of EU customers. What compliance framework applies?
options: GDPR — you must ensure data processing is lawful, data is protected, and the client has a privacy policy

questions.question: After delivering an automation, the client reports it stopped working after they changed their CRM. What should your contract have included?
options: A maintenance clause defining responsibility for updates when dependent systems change, with an agreed support rate

questions.question: What is the correct way to present cost savings to a skeptical CFO?
options: Show conservative, auditable calculations based on current labor costs with before/after comparison — offer a pilot to prove ROI before full investment

questions.question: A client's staff fears AI will replace their jobs. How do you address this during implementation?
options: Ignore the concern — it is not your problem
Involve staff in the automation design, reframe AI as handling tedious tasks so they can focus on higher-value work, and provide training

questions.question: What distinguishes a successful AI consulting engagement from a failed one in Southeast Asia markets?
options: Using the most advanced AI technology
Understanding local business context, building trust through face-to-face relationships, delivering visible quick wins, and ensuring the solution fits the client's actual technical capacity
Completing on time regardless of quality
